<<Economic Stress Analysis>>
The maximum substance abuse incidents occur at an economic stress index of 2, yielding a peak of 13 cases per 1000 people.
To keep incidents below 20, the community must maintain an economic stress index between approximately 0.28 and 3.72.
This economic model is as critical for human safety as water is for a baseball game in the western region.
The calculated range ensures the community stays within safe limits for public health.
<<End of Analysis>>
